title: Capital gains tax
description: How to calculate capital gains tax (CGT) on your assets, assets that are affected, and the CGT discount.

child_links.title: What is capital gains tax?
child_links.description: How capital gains tax (CGT) works, and how you report and pay tax on capital gains when you sell assets.

child_links.title: List of CGT assets and exemptions
child_links.description: Check if your assets are subject to capital gains tax (CGT), exempt, or pre-date CGT.

child_links.title: Acquiring CGT assets
child_links.description: Establish the date you buy or acquire an asset, your share of ownership and records to keep.

child_links.title: CGT events
child_links.description: How and when CGT is triggered, such as when an asset is sold, lost or destroyed.

child_links.title: CGT discount
child_links.description: Find out if your asset is eligible for the 50% CGT discount.

child_links.title: Calculating your CGT
child_links.description: How to calculate your capital gains tax (CGT), including how to use capital losses and work out your cost base.

child_links.title: Property and capital gains tax
child_links.description: How CGT affects real estate, including rental properties, land, improvements and your home.

child_links.title: Shares and similar investments
child_links.description: Find out what can trigger a CGT event, such as selling shares or receiving certain distributions.

child_links.title: Inherited assets and capital gains tax
child_links.description: How and when CGT applies if you sell assets you inherited, including properties and shares.

child_links.title: Foreign residents and capital gains tax
child_links.description: Your tax residency may be different to your residency for other purposes.

child_links.title: Relationship breakdown and capital gains tax
child_links.description: Find out if you can defer, or 'roll over', CGT on assets that transfer to you in a divorce.

child_links.title: Market valuation of assets
child_links.description: You must obtain market valuation of an asset when required by tax law. The valuation must be objective and supportable.
